response.justification: This candidate is a skilled full-stack web engineer with strong communication abilities, but fundamentally misaligned with the role's core requirements. With only ~2 years of C++ experience (vs. 5-10 years required), no Windows platform background, no SQL Server/Oracle expertise, and zero capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge, the technical gaps are too significant. The candidate's 20-year career has consistently moved away from systems programming toward web and application development, the opposite trajectory needed.
strengths: Extensive professional experience (20+ years) demonstrating technical growth and adaptability across multiple languages and domains
Proven ability to communicate technical concepts through teaching, workshops, and public talks at major conferences
Strong software craftsmanship foundation with demonstrated expertise in testing, CI/CD infrastructure, and code quality practices
concerns: C++ experience severely limited (~2 years at National Instruments) versus 5-10 years mandatory requirement; no recent systems programming background
Career entirely in web/application development with no exposure to capital markets, fixed income, investment banking, or financial systems domains
No mentioned experience with Windows platforms, SQL Server, Oracle, or other capital markets-specific technologies (Summit, messaging frameworks)

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise gap (2 years vs 5-10 required)
Zero capital markets or fixed-income domain knowledge
Strong communication and mentoring track record
